summary: Typosquat of @coopeuch design-system scope executes child_process + HTTP exfiltration payload
details: Package name mimics the legitimate @coopeuch design system scope (version jumped to 999.0.4, a common dependency-confusion / overshadowing shape). The tarball ships cb.js and cb-full.js, both of which combine require("child_process") with http.request() POSTing to a hardcoded hostname. This is the canonical shape of a command-exec + outbound HTTP beacon: the module imports the OS shell primitive and an HTTP client, builds a POST request with a hostname literal, and sends data to an external host. The two sibling files (cb.js / cb-full.js) are a staged and full-payload pair typical of beacon/loader packages. There is no legitimate reason for a design-system web package to shell out to child_process and POST to an external host on load. Installing or requiring this package will execute attacker-controlled exfiltration / command handling against the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
